data.description: Treasury Department Issues Statement on G-7 Finance Call

data.title: Sunrun prices $510M securitization, raising over $1.5B in Q3 with 15th securitization since 2015.
data.description: On September 12, 2025, Sunrun, a leading provider of residential battery storage, solar, and home-to-grid power plants in the U.S., revealed the pricing of a securitization of leases and power purchase agreements. This marks Sunrun's fifteenth securitization since 2015 and the fifth issuance in 2025. Throughout the years, the company has established a prominent presence in the market, becoming known as America’s largest provider in this sector.

data.title: Ring Energy CFO Travis T. Thomas resigns to pursue new opportunities. Company assures no disagreements.
data.description: In The Woodlands, Texas, on September 12, 2025, Ring Energy, Inc. (NYSE American: REI) disclosed that its Chief Financial Officer, Travis T. Thomas, has resigned with immediate effect to explore alternate prospects. Mr. Thomas' departure was not due to any conflicts with the company regarding financial or operational aspects.

data.title: Telesat distributes 62% equity of Telesat Lightspeed to subsidiary, no operational changes. (Characters: 89)
data.description: Telesat, a prominent satellite operator, disclosed today the finalization of a substantial equity transfer within its Telesat Lightspeed division. Specifically, 62% of the equity has been successfully distributed to an indirect subsidiary of Telesat Corporation. This subsidiary, entirely owned by the parent companies of Telesat Canada, operates as a non-guarantor within Telesat Canada’s debt agreements. Notably, this transaction does not introduce any operational alterations to the Company.

data.title: ECD Automotive Design to perform 1-for-40 Reverse Stock Split on September 18, 2025. Stock symbol "ECDA," new CUSIP 27877D104.
data.description: ECD Automotive Design, Inc., a prominent Land Rover and Jaguar restoration company, revealed plans for a 1-for-40 reverse stock split of its common stock. This move, effective on September 18, 2025, at market opening time, aims to streamline the company's stock structure. ECD is renowned for its custom luxury creations, such as bespoke Defenders, Range Rovers, Jaguar E-Types, Ford Mustangs, and Toyota FJs. Following this adjustment, ECD's common stock will continue to be listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol "ECDA" with a new CUSIP number 27877D104.

data.title: HII's Ingalls Shipbuilding in Pascagoula hosts annual workshop for nonprofit partners to strengthen community relationships.
data.description: Today in Pascagoula, Mississippi on September 12, 2025, Ingalls Shipbuilding division of HII (NYSE:HII) hosted its third annual Community Partner Workshop. The event aimed to build relationships with nonprofit leaders from the Gulf Coast region. The workshop focused on enhancing collaboration between industry and nonprofits, exchanging best practices, and reinforcing ties within the community.

data.title: Companies like Paxos, Frax, Sky, Agora, and newcomer Native Markets compete for Hyperliquid's USDH with billions at stake.
data.description: Hyperliquid recently held its inaugural stablecoin vote, attracting interest from major players Paxos, Frax, Sky, Agora, and newcomer Native Markets. The vote, which could impact billions in trading volume and stablecoin flows, has garnered significant attention within the industry.

data.title: Itafos to present at Smallcap Discoveries Conference in Vancouver on Sept 29, CEO to discuss recent results and strategies.
data.description: On September 12, 2025, Itafos Inc. (TSX-V: IFOS) (OTCQX: ITFS) disclosed its participation in the forthcoming Annual Smallcap Discoveries Conference in Vancouver. David Delaney, the company's CEO, is scheduled to present on September 29, 2025, discussing Itafos' recent outcomes and future plans.

data.title: Brompton Funds proposes merging Symphony Floating Rate Senior Loan Fund with Brompton Wellington Square Investment Grade CLO ETF. Special meeting to vote on the proposal.
data.description: On September 12, 2025, Brompton Funds Limited, the manager of Symphony Floating Rate Senior Loan Fund (SSF.UN), disclosed their decision to merge the Fund into Brompton Wellington Square Investment Grade CLO ETF. The ETF will serve as the Continuing Fund post-merger, as per the approval of the Manager. A special Meeting has been scheduled for the Unitholders of class A and class U units of the Fund to vote on this proposed Merger. This decision marks a significant transition in the financial landscape, as this strategic move aims to streamline operations and optimize resources effectively for future growth and stability.

Fitch upgrades Italian city of Naples to 'BBB' outlook

data.title: American Cancer Society to hold 40th Anniversary Relay For Life event on Sep 27 at California Lutheran University for 5.0K run in Conejo Valley.
data.description: California Lutheran University hosted a fundraising event that began with a non-competitive 5.0K fun run on September 27, 2025. Taking place in Thousand Oaks, California, the event marked the start of the 2025 Relay For Life of Conejo Valley organized by the American Cancer Society. The event aimed to raise funds for cancer research and support services.

data.title: Tadashi Shoji unveils Spring/Summer 2026 collection at NYFW with Thai-inspired designs. Digital presentation on Sept. 12 at 5PM EST.
data.description: Tadashi Shoji will showcase his Spring/Summer 2026 Ready-to-Wear Collection at New York Fashion Week, timed for September 12, 2025. The digital presentation is set to premiere at 5PM EST (2PM PST) on that day. Drawing inspiration from the Golden Glow of Thailand, the unveiling will feature a pre-recorded video highlighting the latest collection.

data.title: Mars and Unreasonable Group welcome 14 new ventures to Unreasonable Food™ for sustainable food system growth.
data.description: Unreasonable Food, an international network, has the advantage of scaling high-growth ventures that focus on establishing a sustainable food system. In a recent development on September 10, 2025, in New York, Mars and Unreasonable Group have joined forces as part of the Unreasonable Food™ collaboration. This partnership underscores the commitment to foster innovation and sustainability in the food industry on a global scale. With a focus on creating impactful change, this collaboration leverages the combined resources and expertise of both organizations to address the challenges within the food sector. This initiative aims to significantly impact the industry's growth and sustainability metrics, which are expected to drive positive outcomes in the near future.

Fitch upgrades Portugal to 'A' with stable outlook

data.title: Altai's claim for compensatory damages in Quebec court dismissed due to lack of legal representation.
data.description: Altai Resources Inc. (TSXV: ATI) announced on Sept. 12, 2025, that following a news release on Sept. 5, 2025, they are pursuing compensatory damages in connection with their claim filed in 2022 in the Superior Court of Quebec against the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources (Quebec), the Government of Quebec, and the Attorney General of Quebec. This claim is in relation to the expropriation of the Company’s oil and gas exploration assets in Quebec in 2022. The Court has informed Altai that the claim has been dismissed due to the Company lacking legal representation, as requested by the Attorney General of Quebec.

data.title: OpenAI and Microsoft agree on restructuring for-profit arm, seeks approval from CA and DE lawmakers.
data.description: The company indicated that approval from policymakers in California and Delaware would be necessary as a part of the restructuring plan.

data.title: Dogecoin, BNB surge as Bitcoin, Ethereum reach monthly highs. BNB hits new all-time high while Dogecoin, Ethereum, Solana rise.
data.description: On Friday, BNB experienced a record-breaking all-time high price, with significant surges in the value of Dogecoin, Ethereum, and Solana as investors re-entered the cryptocurrency market.
